description: Port the bootstrap phase (overlay/libexec/k3os/bootstrap) into Go. This includes setup_etc (mount tmpfs on /etc, copy files), setup_modules (bind mount kernel modules/firmware), setup_users (create rancher user/group), setup_dirs (create /run/k3os), and setup_config (call k3os config --initrd). Create a new internal/boot/bootstrap package with interface-driven design for testability.
steps: Define a Bootstrap interface in internal/boot/bootstrap/bootstrap.go that abstracts the OS operations needed (FileSystem, Mounter, CommandRunner from internal/iface)

Write tests first in internal/boot/bootstrap/bootstrap_test.go using mock implementations, covering: setup_etc creates /etc and /proc, mounts tmpfs, copies files; setup_modules bind-mounts modules/firmware when they exist; setup_users creates groups and users correctly; setup_dirs creates /run/k3os; setup_config calls k3os config --initrd in non-local mode
Implement Bootstrap struct with Run() method that orchestrates all bootstrap steps in order
Implement each sub-function: SetupEtc, SetupModules, SetupUsers, SetupDirs, SetupConfig
Run golangci-lint run ./... and fix any issues
Run go test ./internal/boot/bootstrap/... and verify all tests pass
Commit with message 'feat(bootstrap): port bootstrap shell script to Go'
acceptance_criteria: internal/boot/bootstrap/ package exists with bootstrap.go and bootstrap_test.go
All bootstrap sub-functions are implemented and tested
Tests use dependency injection (mocked FS, mounter, command runner)
findings: All 18 tests pass. golangci-lint passes with 0 issues. go build and go test ./... both pass. Package uses //go:build linux tag. Bootstrapper struct holds FS, Mounter, Cmd dependencies plus KernelVersion and Mode fields. Run() stops on first error matching set -e behavior. SetupConfig takes mode param from Bootstrapper.Mode field. cp -rfp used for copying /usr/etc to /etc since FileSystem interface does not have a recursive copy method.
